title: Porcelain Mortar and Pestle Set – Pill Crusher, Spice Grinder, Herb Bowl, Pesto Powder – Molcajete for Salt - Plus Lid/Non-Slip Base
description:
About this item
PERFECT HOUSEWARMING GIFT: This Mortar and Pestle is the go-to tool for any home chef serious about scratch cooking. And our finest unglazed porcelain provides the perfect abrasive finish for grinding and crushing
FUNCTIONAL, BEAUTIFUL: This mortar and pestle are crafted of tough, heavy unglazed ceramic and sustainable bamboo wood (pestle handle & coaster). The easy-clean smooth exterior of the mortar is a key point, especially when combined with the grip-friendly long-handled Pestle
MULTI-USE: Designed to be used with dry and wet mixes, this accessory can be used to prepare dry rubs and marinades, grind spices; to create fresh salsa, guacamole, and hummus to your heart’s content
DIMENSIONS: Mortar: 4.7" diam., 1.9" high. Pestle: 4.9" long
DISHWASHER-SAFE: The mortar (not pestle) is dishwasher-safe, but it's best to hand-wash both parts with mild detergent
reviews_5s:
I have done a lot of research and read many reviews regarding marble, porcelain, granite, stainless steel, and glass mortar and pestles. I have to say this was the best one. My purpose for a mortar and pestle was for crushing and finely grinding large and hard supplements. This does the job. I put my left hand over the top partially so nothing jumps out and it works fine. First I tap the tablets straight on to crack the supplements and then grind them down and it works. The wooden base does help to stop it from moving and provides stability. The pestle is light and works well. I do have a singular marble pestle that grinds it even more fine but this is sufficient. I tap the sides and it all comes out easily! No sticking. There may be some staining to the mortar but after cleaning it whatever is left is a permanent stain that won't affect future uses as long as it's been washed thoroughly by hand.

What I read from many types of mortar and pestle reviews and research:
-With marble you have to "prep" the marble so your not grinding the marble and eating it with your food or pills. It takes forever and doesn't always work. I tried this and wish I never bought a marble one. Marble can also grip onto the contents and you can't remove it all- big waste if you have pills.
-With stainless steel - I read it rusts anyway and turns the contents grey
-glass- not strong enough
-granite- although the strongest, can hold onto the pill powder-big waste of medicine or supplement
-Porcelain - the best option but can be tricky- have to get a THICK porcelain and wide mouth (not narrow or too small of a mortar and pestle) so it doesn't crack or break. And this does the trick. I've used this one once and if anything changes I'll write back in.
Also like the wooden base that acts as a cover!
Great price and great quality so far.
Easy to clean with soap and water in sink - DO NOT put any mortar and pestles
in the d/w - bad idea- wouldn't take that risk.

p.s. this would also be great for foods like garlic and guacomole-may need a little large for a large amount of dip but otherwise take in stages. perfect for pills, supplements, and herbs!

Enjoy! Hope this helps!I am very happy with this purchase of the Mortar and Pestle. Excellent quality and well thought out design. It has rough texture inside which is exactly what you want and the pestle is sturdy and fits well in the hand. The size is perfect. I can grind spices or make aioli in it with ease.Made well and serves its purpose and doesn't take up a lot of reality on the counter. Overall very nice.When you need to crush a pill or spice seed, this will do it.Excellent quality, great priceGot this a few months ago, works just as we need it to. Have had no issues.He uses it to crush certain tablets he takes every day. It helps with the swallowing issues.This product was better than what I expected - both in quality and size. It was also surprisingly well packaged!!I've had many pestle and mortars in my life, but this is my favorite. The size is perfect and bigger than most small sets, and I love how the texture of the ceramic helps grab things so you can get a better grind in more easily. The cherry on top of literally the lid for the top of it, so things can be saved for later, or kept from dying out during prep time.Dice your onions tiny for this mortar and pestle.
reviews_1s:
Just like the granite or other stone pestal/mortar sets
THIS LEAVES DUST IN FOOD

IF YOU LIKE EATING ROCK DUST AND BREAKING YOUR TEETH AND CAUSING YOURSELF HEALTH PROBLEMS THEN BUY THIS ------ OR BE SMART AND BUY A STAINLESS MORTAR AND PESTLE

DO NOT BUY THIS - DOESNT MATTER HOW MUCH YOU CLEAN OR "SET" IT - IT WILL CONSTANTLY LEAVE DUST .... IDK WWHO WOULD EVEN MAKE THIS AND SELL IT AS A FOOD PREP ITEM - IT CREATES DUST !Only the outside of the mortar is smooth. The inside of the mortar and the pestle are rough. The sound it makes is similar to nails on a black board. Painful. Moreover releases porcelain dust.Everything sticks to the pestle! I think I lose half the dosage because I can't even scape it off. It seems that whatever you are grinding, grinds deep into the pestle. Clearly this was designed with the wrong material. The rounded edge of the pestle should be smooth and sealed against incorporating material in the bow!I made some pesto and crushed some basils and the mortar cracked within 5 mins of its very first use. It's either some poor quality or a defect item. Please refund or replace.The porcelain scratched with the first use. Cleaning was a pain. It’s not worth it.Nice except it was missing the base. Cost more to send back and get complete replacement than worth. No option to just have the missing base sent.Poor product in all categories. Very disappointedBroke after the second useit brokei purchased this a few months ago. the pestle came unglued from the handle after only using it a few times. it doesn't grind as well as my old marble one. I would not recommend this product
